title: Adaptive VaR Control for Standardized Option Books under Marking Frictions
summary: Develops a tail-risk framework for standardized option books that combines a base conditional-quantile forecast with sequential conformal recalibration. Tested on SPX option data 2018-2025; the uncalibrated base systematically underestimates downside risk, and sequential recalibration restores coverage and improves rolling-window tail stability. Provides an approximate one-step exceedance-control result and quantifies the error from next-day marking when same-contract quotes are unavailable.
key_insight: Sequential conformal recalibration on top of a base conditional-quantile forecast is a tractable way to keep VaR coverage honest on option-book tails through regime shifts, including the next-day marking-friction problem when same-contract quotes vanish.

title: Measuring Strategy-Decay Risk: Minimum Regime Performance and the Durability of Systematic Investing
summary: Introduces Minimum Regime Performance (MRP) — the lowest realized risk-adjusted return across distinct historical regimes — as a lower bound on a systematic strategy's durability. Applied to a broad universe of factor strategies, MRP exposes an efficiency-vs-resilience trade-off: high long-term Sharpe does not imply high MRP. Code on GitHub at github.com/nolanalexander/minimum_regime_performance.
key_insight: MRP turns 'strategy decay' from anecdote into a regime-conditional measurable that complements market and liquidity risk and helps rank factor strategies by robustness rather than by mean Sharpe.

title: Skewness Dispersion and Stock Market Returns
authors: Mykola Babiak, Jozef Barunik, Josef Kurka
summary: Cross-sectional dispersion in firm-level realized skewness is significantly and negatively related to future stock market returns. The signal's predictive content is incremental to a comprehensive set of 50 established predictors in both in-sample and out-of-sample tests; only six other variables beat that bar.
key_insight: Cross-sectional skewness dispersion is a previously-overlooked aggregate market-return predictor that survives a horse race against most of the predictor zoo, including in OOS tests.

title: lob-regime-scanner: HMM-based market microstructure regime detection for cryptocurrency order books
summary: End-to-end open-source pipeline that ingests Tardis L2 snapshots, computes 30+ microstructure features (multi-level OFI, VPIN, Kyle's lambda, spread/vol/autocorr), fits a 3-state Gaussian HMM (selected by BIC across K=2..5), decodes regimes via Viterbi, and visualizes everything in a 4-panel Plotly Dash dashboard. C++/pybind11 LOB engine handles 1M+ updates/sec; 158-test pytest suite. Reports a regime-conditional strategy with claimed Sharpe 1.8-2.5 (test set, walk-forward).
key_insight: Demonstrates a fully reproducible HMM-on-microstructure regime classifier with a working dashboard architecture — the kind of reference design to mine for OFI/VPIN feature definitions and a real-time regime overlay pattern, even if the headline Sharpe is treated skeptically (only 3 stars, no out-of-sample period beyond train/test split).

title: On options-driven realized volatility forecasting: Information gains via rough volatility model
authors: Zheqi Fan, Meng (Melody) Wang, Yifan Ye
summary: Extracts model-based spot volatility from traded options under a rough stochastic volatility model (iterative two-step estimation a la Andersen et al. 2015a) and feeds it into the HAR family. Benchmarked against Heston, Bates, SVCJ and the VIX, the augmented HAR-RV-RHeston improves daily realized-volatility forecasting and sustains it. A deep-learning surrogate accelerates large-panel estimation.
key_insight: Spot volatility extracted from options under a rough-vol model carries forecast information that the VIX (and standard SV models) miss — a candidate input feature for HAR-style RV forecasters in a quantamental risk engine.
